patient.name: Lucas Rodriguez
symptoms: crusty scabs that come and go
patient.medical_history: no significant past medical history, experiencing a mild cold recently

timeline.summary: Triaged as SEMI_URGENT. Patient presenting with red spots on the skin and itching around the mouth. Sent to doctor for evaluation.

details.triage_reasoning: Patient presents with skin symptoms (red spots and itching), slightly swollen lymph nodes, and a recent mild cold, indicating possible allergic reaction or infection. No critical symptoms noted but requires follow-up within 30-60 minutes for evaluation and treatment.
details.notes: Patient has no significant past medical history and is not on medications, suggesting a currently stable condition.
details.context_for_next: Evaluate for possible allergic reaction or infection.
details.viewer_output: Triaged as SEMI_URGENT. Patient presenting with red spots on the skin and itching around the mouth. Sent to doctor for evaluation.

timeline.summary: Ordering blood work from General Lab to assess for any inflammatory markers or signs of infection or allergy. Found 3 relevant past cases suggesting this approach.

details.context_for_next: to assess for any inflammatory markers or signs of infection/allergy
details.reasoning: Ordering blood work to assess for any inflammatory markers or signs of infection/allergy is warranted due to possible allergic reaction or infection indicated by the patient's symptoms. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes related to allergic reactions without prior tests.
details.learning_principles_used: Used case #1: Patient successfully diagnosed with allergic reaction without tests.

timeline.summary: Blood work completed. Results sent to your doctor.

timeline.summary: Diagnosis: allergic reaction. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: Antihistamines for symptom relief.

details.recommended_treatment: Antihistamines for symptom relief and oral corticosteroids if needed for inflammation.
details.context_for_next: Patient exhibits signs consistent with an allergic reaction.
details.reasoning: Found 3 similar cases successfully diagnosed with allergic reactions. Labs show elevated WBC and neutrophils, suggesting possible inflammatory or allergic response. Confident in diagnosis based on context and past outcomes.
details.learning_principles_used: Used case #1: allergic reaction diagnosis successful outcome.
details.viewer_output: Diagnosis: allergic reaction. Found 3 similar cases with successful outcomes. Treatment: Antihistamines for symptom relief.

timeline.summary: Learned: Red spots with crusty scabs require swab testing for impetigo diagnosis. This will improve future infection detection.

details.principle_text: Red spots on the skin with crusty scabs requires swab testing to confirm impetigo instead of assuming an allergic reaction.
details.reasoning: The initial diagnosis of allergic reaction overlooked the characteristic features of impetigo, leading to missed infection treatment.
details.viewer_output: Learned: Red spots with crusty scabs require swab testing for impetigo diagnosis. This will improve future infection detection.
